chunks.text: The Silk Road, from ancient times
connected Asia in the east and Europe in the west.
It was not a single road, but a network of many route.
Chinese silk, Persian glass, Indian spice, and Roman gold traveled this road by caravan.
Merchant loaded their camels and crossed the desert, crossed the mountains.
But it was not only goods that traveled this road.
Religion also spread along this path.
Buddhism spread from India to China; Islam spread from Arabia into Central Asia.
Language and art were also exchanged.
The Chinese way of making paper traveled westward and would later support the culture of Europe.
The Silk Road was humanity's first great attempt at connection.
The words carried by the desert wind, over a thousand years
still quietly support the roots of our civilization.
